concepts.definition: A temporary container where customers collect products before purchasing.
The process of completing a purchase by providing shipping and payment details.
A saved list of products a customer intends to purchase in the future.
The action of placing a product into the shopping cart for future purchase.
A shortcut action to immediately purchase a product without adding it to the cart first.
A page displaying available products with key details such as name, price, and image.
A hierarchical grouping used to organize products by type or purpose.
A mechanism to narrow search results by specific attributes such as size, color, or price range.
The ability to order search results or product lists by a specific criterion such as price or relevance.
The functionality allowing customers to find products by entering keywords or phrases.
The act of exploring products without a specific purchase intent.
Products suggested to a customer based on their browsing history, preferences, or purchase patterns.
A product that ranks among the highest in sales volume within a given period.
A recently added product to the catalog.
A sale event to reduce remaining inventory of discontinued or seasonal products.
A group of products sold together at a combined price, typically at a discount.
A prepaid card with a stored monetary value that can be redeemed for purchases.
Reward credits earned through purchases that can be redeemed for discounts or perks.
An online platform where multiple sellers list and sell their products to buyers.
The front-facing interface of an online store where customers browse and purchase products.
The monetary amount required to purchase a product or service.
A reduction in the original price of a product, expressed as a percentage or fixed amount.
A unique alphanumeric code that provides a price reduction when applied at checkout.
A marketing activity designed to increase sales through special offers or incentives.
A time-limited sale event offering steep discounts to create urgency.
The physical delivery of purchased goods from seller to customer.
The act of transporting a package to its final destination.
A unique identifier assigned to a shipment for monitoring its transit status.
A company responsible for transporting packages from origin to destination.
A large facility used for storing goods before they are shipped to customers.
The process of receiving, processing, and delivering orders to customers.
A prepaid shipping label provided to a customer for returning a product.
Government-imposed duties and regulations on goods crossing international borders.
The transport of goods in bulk, typically by sea, air, rail, or road.
The act of sending out a shipment from the warehouse for delivery.
The final stage of delivery from the distribution hub to the customer's door.
A premium shipping option with faster transit times and guaranteed delivery dates.
The default shipping method with regular transit times and lower cost.
A designated location where customers can collect their packages in person.
A major logistics hub where goods are sorted and routed to local delivery networks.
A legal agreement outlining the rules and guidelines for using a service or product.
A document describing how an organization collects, uses, and protects personal data.

terms.text: プライバシーポリシー

concepts.definition: The rules governing how customers can return products and receive their money back.
A guarantee that a product will function as described for a specified period.
Legal responsibility for damages, losses, or obligations arising from use of a product or service.
A contractual obligation to compensate for losses or damages incurred by another party.
Legal rights over creations of the mind such as inventions, designs, and brand names.
An obligation to keep certain information secret and not disclose it to third parties.
Adherence to laws, regulations, and industry standards that govern business operations.
Safeguards and practices ensuring personal data is collected, stored, and used lawfully.
Explicit agreement from a user to allow processing of their personal data or acceptance of terms.
A method of resolving disputes outside of court through a neutral third party.
The jurisdiction and legal framework that applies to a contract or agreement.
A clause excusing parties from contractual obligations due to extraordinary and unforeseeable events.
A clause that caps the maximum amount of damages a party can be held responsible for.
The department responsible for assisting customers with inquiries, issues, and complaints.
A collection of answers to the most common questions customers ask.
A formal request for help submitted by a customer, tracked through a ticketing system.
The process of transferring an unresolved issue to a higher level of support or management.
The successful conclusion of a support case where the customer's issue has been addressed.
A service level agreement defining expected response times and performance standards.
A centralized repository of articles and guides to help users find solutions independently.
A real-time messaging feature enabling customers to communicate with support agents.
A service where the support team returns a customer's call at a scheduled time.
Information provided by customers about their experience with a product or service.
A questionnaire sent to customers to measure their satisfaction with support interactions.
A centralized online portal where customers can find support resources and contact options.
A systematic process of diagnosing and resolving technical problems step by step.

terms.text: トラブルシューティング

concepts.definition: A formal report submitted when a user encounters a software defect or unexpected behavior.
A suggestion from a user for a new capability or improvement to the product.
A coordinated set of marketing activities aimed at achieving a specific business goal.
A periodic email communication sent to subscribers with updates, offers, or content.
A prominent visual advertisement displayed on a webpage to attract user attention.
A prompt designed to encourage a user to take a specific action such as signing up or purchasing.
A standalone web page created specifically for a marketing campaign to drive conversions.
The percentage of users who complete a desired action out of the total who had the opportunity.
The level of interaction and involvement users have with content, brand, or platform.
The identity, reputation, and perception of a company in the minds of customers.
The practice of optimizing web content to rank higher in search engine results.
Online platforms that enable users to create and share content and interact with communities.
A person with a significant online following who can affect purchasing decisions of their audience.
A marketing arrangement where a partner earns commission for driving sales or traffic.
The process of identifying and cultivating potential customers for a business.
An experiment comparing two variants of a page or element to determine which performs better.
A strategy of creating and distributing valuable content to attract and retain a target audience.

terms.text: コンテンツマーケティング

concepts.definition: An application programming interface that enables communication between software systems.
A software development kit providing tools, libraries, and documentation for building applications.
The connection of two or more systems to exchange data and work together.
An automated HTTP callback triggered by a specific event in a source system.
The process of verifying the identity of a user or system attempting to access a resource.
The process of determining what actions an authenticated user is permitted to perform.
A specific URL where an API receives requests for a particular resource or operation.
A restriction on the number of API requests a client can make within a given time period.
The data content transmitted in the body of an API request or response.
A numeric code returned by an API indicating the result of a request.
The time delay between a request being sent and the response being received.
The percentage of time a system is operational and accessible over a given period.
The process of releasing and installing a new version of software into an environment.
An isolated testing environment that mirrors production but uses simulated data and services.
The live environment where the application serves real users and processes real data.
